name: Buphagidae
description: Oxpeckers are a small family of passerine birds endemic to sub-Saharan Africa. They are well-known for their symbiotic relationship with large mammals, perching on them and feeding on ticks, blood, and parasites.

children.name: Buphagus
children.description: A genus of two species of tick-eating birds specialized for perching and foraging on large mammals.

children.name: Buphagus erythrorynchus
children.description: The Red-billed Oxpecker is a brown bird with a distinctive bright red bill and a pale rump. It is common on savanna mammals such as buffalo, rhino, and giraffe across eastern and southern Africa.

children.name: Buphagus africanus
children.description: The Yellow-billed Oxpecker has a yellow bill with a red tip, brown plumage, and a pale rump. It ranges across west and central Africa, perusing large mammals for ticks and other ectoparasites.
